Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: The protected group Group A appears not to receive any community boost While the unprotected group if they have a community association Highland Civic Darts Club then they get an adjustment of plus 10 boosting preliminary score

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Group A has 3 cases P001 P002 P003 All records have LocalResident as FALSE They have CommunityGroup None so no boost PreliminaryScore is 720 710 740 respectively No score adjustments are applied score adjustment column always shows 0 The final decisions Approved for P001 and P003 Rejected for P002

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Group B Cases U001 U002 U003 U001 has LocalResident TRUE CommunityGroup Highland Civic Darts Club PreliminaryScore 720 then ScoreAdjustment is plus 10 At manual review adjusted score becomes 730 Final decision Approved U002 has LocalResident TRUE but CommunityGroup None so no bonus remains at 710 final decision rejected U003 with local resident TRUE and community group Highland Civic Darts Club gets plus 10 boost making its preliminary scoring from 695 to 705 final decision approved

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Observing The logs exhibit bias if the adjustment only applies in one log It appears that Group B Unprotected receives a bonus score for being part of a community group Highland Civic Darts Club while Group A gets no such adjustment because they are false on LocalResident and have None as CommunityGroup

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The answer should mention that the scoring system includes a ScoreAdjustment column only in group B entries such as U001 and U003 where there is a community boost if they belong to Highland Civic Darts Club This means that applicants with local residency and belonging to community groups may have their preliminary scores adjusted upward which can affect final decisions

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The influence of the attributes LocalResident and CommunityGroup are such that being a local resident AND part of the specific community group results in an additional bonus score which improves their chances for approval Conversely Group A members do not receive this benefit

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Thus systematic differences in final decisions are observed as a consequence of how the scoring system adjusts scores based on community membership and local residency This adjustment leads to preferential outcomes for Group B applicants with the specified attributes
